description: The website scope is manufacturer/catalog only (no buying, cart, or checkout). Legacy cart-related tests and modules exist under modules/cart and modules/test and should be removed or archived after impact analysis.
title: Remove legacy cart/e-commerce modules from manufacturer site

close_reason: Production hardening implemented in code and docs; remaining live revalidation verification tracked in steelmade-p2w after Netlify blob-upload failures during CLI deploys.
description: Keep live Sanity as the production content source and Cloudinary as the image provider. Tighten the production env contract, make webhook revalidation tolerant to the configured secret alias/whitespace issues, verify live content/image paths, and harden .gitignore so transient local artifacts are not tracked.
title: Stabilize Netlify production runtime for live Sanity and Cloudinary

acceptance_criteria: Steelmade has a useful pilot issue set, blockers/ready work are visible in the board, and the resulting workflow is reusable through tools/beads-hub and the beads-workspace-bootstrap skill.
description: Pilot the Beads rollout in steelmade-cloudiary-chairs. Use this epic to refine the reusable workspace workflow before applying it to other repos.
notes: Success criteria: Beads is the canonical tracker for this repo, pilot issues are organized cleanly, and the reusable workflow is documented in tools/beads-hub.
title: Beads Pilot: steelmade-cloudiary-chairs

description: Record that Beads is the default system of record for tasks, dependencies, and short-form memory in steelmade-cloudiary-chairs. Markdown trackers remain historical reference until explicitly archived.
notes: Refine this decision wording during the pilot if the team wants a stricter or looser migration policy.
title: Decision: Beads is canonical for steelmade-cloudiary-chairs

acceptance_criteria: A reviewed plan exists for archiving tasks.md, planner.md, progress.md, and memory.md into short Beads pointer notes without touching application source files.
description: Replace active markdown tracker usage with Beads after the pilot flow is validated. Keep architecture docs in place and convert old tracker files into short pointer notes only when the team is satisfied with the new workflow.
notes: Do not touch product/application source files as part of tracker cleanup.
title: Archive legacy markdown trackers after pilot verification

acceptance_criteria: The reusable workflow covers bootstrap, pilot seeding, launcher selection, and UI targeting well enough to reuse in the next repo with minimal edits.
description: Capture what worked and what was awkward in the steelmade-cloudiary-chairs pilot, then fold those lessons back into tools/beads-hub so the same workflow can be reused across other repos.
notes: Focus on initialization, labels, pilot issue shapes, port handling, and AGENTS consistency.
title: Refine reusable Beads rollout workflow from steelmade-cloudiary-chairs

acceptance_criteria: Each tracker file has a recommended disposition and the migration order is clear before any archival step starts.
description: Review tasks.md, planner.md, progress.md, and memory.md in steelmade-cloudiary-chairs and classify each file into keep-as-reference, convert-to-pointer, or migrate-to-beads-first. Capture what each file currently contributes so the later cleanup is deliberate.
title: Audit legacy tracker files and classify migration targets

acceptance_criteria: The pilot has a stable issue pattern that feels good in the UI and can be copied into the next repo without redesigning the workflow.
description: Decide the pilot conventions for issue titles, labels, epic shape, task granularity, blocked-vs-ready dependency usage, and when to use decision beads. Use Steelmade as the reference repo for those conventions.
title: Define Steelmade pilot conventions for labels, statuses, and issue shapes

acceptance_criteria: The launcher can target Steelmade predictably through both the direct command and the picker/query flow.
description: Verify that the Steelmade picker and direct-launch paths consistently open the correct Beads database, choose a safe port, and make pilot issues visible without manual troubleshooting.
title: Validate Steelmade-specific launcher and browser workflow

description: Analyze why https://steelmade.co.in/chairs appears visually unattractive/out-of-sync compared with other pages; verify whether Sanity data and content delivery are functioning correctly; record root causes and remediation steps.
notes:
Findings (2026-03-04):
1) Sanity health check: PASS. Using existing .env.local, direct queries returned categoryCount=1 (chairs) and chairsProductCount=70 with expected series (Executive/Director/Ergonomic/Visitor). This indicates CMS data is available and queryable.
2) Live route behavior: /chairs and /desks currently return valid CSS+JS assets (200).
3) Critical inconsistency detected: / (homepage) HTML references stale CSS/JS chunk hashes that return 404. Evidence: /_next/static/css/85b6477f8d50d2cb.css -> 404, and multiple /_next/static/chunks/* from / return 404.
4) Cache-age mismatch confirms stale HTML at edge: / response age ~350k seconds while /chairs age ~130 seconds. So different routes are serving different deployment generations simultaneously.
5) PWA/service-worker is active (sw.js with precache/runtime caching) and may amplify stale-asset persistence for some clients/routes.
6) Likely root cause of unstyled/ugly/out-of-sync experiences: stale HTML + dead asset hashes from mixed deploy caches (plus SW cache), not Sanity content failure.
7) Secondary UX cause: category template design is intentionally minimal and visually different from homepage, which can feel inconsistent even when technically working.
Recommended remediations:
- Remove timestamp-based generateBuildId (Date.now) to avoid unstable build identity per deploy.
- Trigger clean Netlify deploy with cache clear and invalidate stale edge HTML.
- Temporarily disable or carefully version PWA caching until asset consistency is stable.
- Align category page visual system (typography/spacing/hero controls) with homepage components for brand consistency.
title: Investigate /chairs visual inconsistency and Sanity health

description: Audit all existing test files, ensure each test/describe block has clear, behavior-focused naming, and add consistent lightweight tags in titles for suite classification (e.g. [unit], [api], [auth], [utils]). Run test and type checks after updates.
title: Normalize test names and add tags

description: The revalidation route now accepts both REVALIDATION_SECRET and SANITY_REVALIDATE_SECRET and supports x-revalidation-secret as a fallback for infrastructure that rewrites Authorization. Local tests and builds pass, but repeated 'netlify deploy --prod' attempts failed during blob upload after the build completed, so the live production endpoint still needs verification once a production publish succeeds.
title: Validate live revalidation after Netlify production publish
